staticText: Ghana SSNIT 2026 calculator. From 1 January 2026, monthly insurable earnings are bounded at GHS 587.80 minimum and GHS 69,000 maximum. Worker contribution is 5.5% and employer contribution is 13% of monthly basic salary, totaling 18.5%. The combined amount is allocated 13.5% to mandatory Tier 1 SSNIT and 5% to mandatory Tier 2. Within the 13.5% Tier 1 remittance, 2.5% is allocated to NHIA and 11% is retained by SSNIT; NHIA is not an extra contribution and must not be added again. Old-age pension under Act 766 requires at least 180 credited months. Pension right is 37.5% at 180 months plus 0.09375 percentage points for every additional credited month, capped at 60% at 420 months. At age 60, a planning estimate applies the pension right to the user-supplied average monthly salary for the best 36 months. Do not calculate a payable pension for retirement ages 55-59 without the applicable SSNIT early-age reduction factor. Do not invent Tier 2 balances or returns, Tier 3 returns, survivor or invalidity benefits, emigration benefits, arrears, penalties, interest, refunds or contribution history. Below 180 months, refer the old-age lump-sum amount to SSNIT because actual contributions and interest records are required. Sources: SSNIT 2026 minimum/maximum insurable-earnings notice; SSNIT member and employer guidance; SSNIT old-age benefit guidance; National Pensions Act 2008 (Act 766). The calculator is local-only and is not an SSNIT record, filing, award or advice.
